# Poultry Farm Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-poultry-farm-poultry-farm-site  
**Vertical slug:** poultry-farm  
**Niche family:** NF-AGR-LIV (anchor)  
**Family role:** anchor  
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's poultry sector produces over 300,000 metric tonnes of chicken and 650,000 metric tonnes of eggs annually, making it the largest in sub-Saharan Africa. Over 180,000 poultry farms operate across Nigeria, concentrated in Ogun, Oyo, Kano, Kaduna, and FCT. The sector is dominated by small-to-medium farms (500–50,000 birds) selling broilers, layers (egg production), and day-old chicks (DOC). Most farms sell via WhatsApp broadcast lists, Jumia Business, or roadside kiosks. Prices fluctuate with feed costs (primarily maize and soya from AFEX, NIRSAL); live broiler ₦4,500–₦8,000 per kg dressed weight; eggs ₦2,200–₦3,000 per crate (30 eggs).

## 2. Target Audience Profile
**Persona A — Chukwuemeka Nwachukwu, 38, Ibadan**: Medium-scale farmer (5,000 birds), wants a website to attract bulk buyers from hotels, restaurants (B2B) and reduce reliance on middlemen. WhatsApp is his current channel.
**Persona B — Mrs Abiodun Fashola, 45, Lagos**: Restaurant owner in Surulere. Wants to source directly from a trusted farm for consistent weekly supply of dressed chicken. Searches Google for "poultry farm near me Lagos".
**Persona C — Ibrahim Sule, 29, Kaduna**: Small investor wanting to buy 1,000 day-old chicks to start his own farm. Needs a trustworthy supplier with NAFDAC-registered vaccines and verified mortality rates.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
**Home:** Hero (farm name, "Fresh from Farm to Table" tagline, WhatsApp order CTA), product cards (broilers, layers, eggs, DOC, dressed chicken — price per kg/unit in ₦), trust strip (NAFDAC registration, years in operation, bird capacity), farm location, delivery coverage.
**About:** Farm history, capacity, biosecurity practices, feed management, certifications.
**Services/Products:** Full price list, bulk pricing, delivery terms, minimum order quantities.
**Contact:** WhatsApp order CTA, phone, address, contact form with NDPR.

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Professional but approachable — farmer-to-market trust. "Fresh, healthy, farm-to-table" positioning. Language: Nigerian business English. Primary CTA: "Order via WhatsApp." Secondary: "Request Bulk Pricing." AVOID: stock images of Western industrial farms; use Nigerian farm environments.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
NAFDAC registration for vaccines/feed additives; NAFDAC number displayed; years operating; bird capacity badge ("10,000 birds weekly capacity"); VAN (Veterinary Association Nigeria) vet on-call; feed certification (Premier Feed, Hybrid, Top Feed); client testimonials (restaurant/hotel buyers); CAC registration; delivery coverage map (Ogun, Lagos, Ibadan etc.).

## 6. Image Direction
1. Nigerian farmer in green overalls inspecting healthy white broiler chickens in a clean deep-litter house, Ogun State farm setting.
2. Fresh dressed chicken laid out for sale — clean presentation, market display, warm lighting.
3. Rows of egg-laying hens in battery cages — modern Nigerian farm infrastructure.
4. Day-old chicks (DOC) huddled under heat lamp — close-up, healthy yellow chicks on clean litter.
5. Delivery: farm worker loading plastic crates of eggs onto a pickup truck with farm logo.
6. Bulk dressed chicken in freezer — restaurant/institutional client supply context.
7. Farm owner (Nigerian man in polo shirt) standing proudly at farm entrance with "Welcome to [Farm Name]" signage.

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
NAFDAC: vaccines and feed additives must be NAFDAC-registered. State Ministry of Agriculture: biosecurity compliance. FMARD: feedmill certification if processing own feed. Meat processing requires NAFDAC food processing number. VAN vet certification display recommended.

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| WhatsApp CTA home + contact | ✅ REQUIRED |
| All prices in ₦ | ✅ REQUIRED (per kg, per crate, per DOC) |
| Nigerian cities | ✅ Ibadan, Lagos, Kaduna, Ogun, FCT |
| Nigerian names | ✅ Chukwuemeka, Ibrahim, Abiodun |
| NAFDAC trust signal | ✅ MANDATORY |
| Paystack / bank transfer / POS | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Mobile-first 375px | ✅ REQUIRED |
| NDPR consent | ✅ REQUIRED |
